questions.question: By 1914, the Scramble for Africa had resulted in nearly the entire continent being colonized. Which two African nations remained fully independent?
questions.explanation: By 1914, only Ethiopia and Liberia remained independent African nations. Ethiopia defeated Italy at the Battle of Adwa in 1896 — one of the most significant victories of any African nation over a European colonial power — preserving its sovereignty. Liberia, founded in 1822 by freed American slaves and recognized as a republic in 1847, maintained independence partly through American diplomatic support. The rest of Africa was divided among Britain, France, Germany, Belgium, Portugal, Italy, and Spain.
questions.memory_hook: 1914: only Ethiopia and Liberia free in Africa. Ethiopia beat Italy at Adwa (1896). Liberia founded by freed American slaves. Entire continent otherwise carved up by European powers.

questions.question: Which country launched the first human into space in 1961?
questions.explanation: Soviet cosmonaut Yuri Gagarin became the first human to travel to space on April 12, 1961, completing one orbit of Earth in 108 minutes aboard Vostok 1. His flight proved that humans could survive in space. The US sent its first astronaut (Alan Shepard) into space 23 days later, though only on a brief suborbital flight. Gagarin died in a training accident in 1968.
questions.memory_hook: Gagarin = first human in space, April 12, 1961. 108 minutes, one orbit. He said 'Poyekhali!' (Let's go!) when the rocket launched. He became a global celebrity overnight and never piloted a spacecraft again — he was too valuable as a symbol to risk.

questions.question: The Hundred Years War finally ended in 1453, the same year as another major historical event. What was it?
questions.explanation: 1453 was remarkable for two world-changing events: the end of the Hundred Years War and the fall of Constantinople to the Ottoman Turks (May 29, 1453). The fall of Constantinople ended the Byzantine Empire (Eastern Roman Empire) and shifted the balance of power in Europe and the Middle East. Many historians consider it the symbolic end of the Middle Ages.
questions.memory_hook: 1453 = Hundred Years War ends AND Constantinople falls to the Ottomans. Two world-changing events in one year. The end of medieval Europe happens twice simultaneously. The Byzantine Empire had kept Roman civilization alive for 1,000 years — gone in 1453.

questions.question: In figure skating, what is the name of the jump that takes off from the back outside edge and lands on the back outside edge of the same foot?
questions.explanation: The loop is a figure skating jump that takes off from the back outside edge of one foot and lands on the same edge of the same foot. It is one of the six standard jumps in figure skating. The Axel is the only jump that takes off from a forward edge, making it the most difficult standard jump. The Lutz takes off from the back outside edge of the opposite foot.
questions.memory_hook: Loop jump = takes off and lands on the same foot's back outside edge. The Axel is the hardest because it's the only one with a forward takeoff — you jump forward, rotate, land backward. A triple Axel = 3.5 rotations in the air.

questions.question: Which East African country contains Mount Kilimanjaro — the highest peak in Africa at 5,895 meters — and is also home to the Serengeti National Park?
questions.explanation: Mount Kilimanjaro is located in northeastern Tanzania, near the Kenyan border. The Serengeti National Park, famous for the annual Great Migration of wildebeest and zebras, is also in Tanzania. The country also contains Zanzibar and part of Lake Victoria, Africa's largest lake.
questions.memory_hook: Tanzania = Kilimanjaro + Serengeti. East Africa's crown jewels.

questions.question: What is the highest possible score per frame in bowling?
questions.explanation: The maximum score in a single bowling frame is 30 points, achieved by rolling three consecutive strikes beginning in that frame (or having three strikes in the 10th frame). In the 10th (final) frame, a bowler who gets a strike is allowed two additional rolls. Three strikes in the 10th frame equals 30 points for that frame. This is how a perfect 300 game is built — 10 frames of 30.
questions.memory_hook: Max frame score = 30 (three strikes). 10 frames of 30 = perfect 300. In the 10th frame you get up to 3 balls if you keep striking. The 300 game is 12 consecutive strikes because of those bonus rolls in the final frame.

questions.question: The 'Godfather of Soul,' known for songs like 'I Got You (I Feel Good),' was which legendary artist?
questions.explanation: James Brown earned the title 'Godfather of Soul' for his revolutionary contributions to funk, soul, and R&B music. His tight, rhythmically complex style influenced hip-hop, funk, and dance music. 'Please Please Please,' 'It's a Man's Man's Man's World,' 'Get Up (I Feel Like Being a) Sex Machine,' and 'I Got You' are among his classics. James Brown performed energetically into his 70s and is one of the most sampled artists in history.
questions.memory_hook: James Brown = Godfather of Soul. 'I Feel Good,' 'Sex Machine.' The most sampled artist in history — hip-hop was built partly on his beats. His famous cape routine: he collapses from exhaustion, an aide throws a cape over him, he throws it off and keeps performing.

questions.question: Which 2014 Marvel film surprised critics and audiences to become a massive hit, featuring a talking raccoon and a living tree?
questions.explanation: Guardians of the Galaxy (2014), directed by James Gunn, featured Rocket Raccoon (voiced by Bradley Cooper), Groot (Vin Diesel saying only 'I am Groot'), Chris Pratt's Peter Quill/Star-Lord, and others. Few expected a film about obscure comic characters to work. It earned $773 million worldwide and is often cited as the MCU's most unexpectedly successful film.
questions.memory_hook: Guardians = nobody expected it to work. A talking raccoon, a tree that says three words, and a cast of D-list comic characters. It made $773 million and launched the cosmic MCU. 'I am Groot' became a cultural catchphrase. Vin Diesel recorded the line in 15 languages.

questions.question: 'Bohemian Rhapsody,' the biographical film about Freddie Mercury and Queen, won how many Academy Awards in 2019?
questions.explanation: Bohemian Rhapsody won four Academy Awards at the 91st Academy Awards in 2019: Best Film Editing, Best Sound Editing, Best Sound Mixing, and Best Actor (Rami Malek as Freddie Mercury). Despite mixed critical reception, the film was a massive box office success, earning over $900 million worldwide. Rami Malek's transformation into Freddie Mercury was widely praised.
questions.memory_hook: Bohemian Rhapsody won 4 Oscars, including Rami Malek for Best Actor. Critics were mixed; audiences loved it. $900 million worldwide. Malek wore custom prosthetics to replicate Freddie's distinctive teeth. He trained for months to replicate Mercury's movements.

questions.question: The Sahara Desert covers approximately what percentage of Africa?
questions.explanation: The Sahara Desert covers approximately 9.2 million square kilometers, making up about 25-30% of Africa's total land area of about 30.4 million square kilometers. Despite its enormous size, the Sahara is sparsely populated — fewer than 2.5 million people live there, mostly in oasis settlements. About 25% of the Sahara is sand (ergs); the rest is rocky desert (hamada) and gravel plains.
questions.memory_hook: Sahara = about 25% of Africa. As big as the United States. Mostly rock and gravel, not sand — only 25% is the classic sand dunes people picture. The Sahara was green and wet 10,000 years ago. Ancient rock art shows hippos and giraffes where there's now just sand.

questions.question: What is the term for the minimum amount of energy needed to start a chemical reaction?
questions.explanation: Activation energy is the minimum energy required to initiate a chemical reaction. Even reactions that release energy (exothermic) need an initial input of activation energy to get started — like how you need to light a match to start a fire. Catalysts (including enzymes in living organisms) work by lowering the activation energy needed, allowing reactions to proceed at lower temperatures or more quickly.
questions.memory_hook: Activation energy = energy to start a reaction. Even reactions that release energy need a push to start. Enzymes in your body lower activation energy so reactions happen at body temperature rather than needing extreme heat. You are chemistry running at 37 degrees.

questions.question: What do enzymes do in biological systems, and what happens to enzyme function when temperature gets too high?
options: They store energy; high temperature makes them more efficient
They break down cell walls; high temperature makes them stronger
They are biological catalysts that speed up chemical reactions; excessive heat denatures them, destroying their function
They carry oxygen; high temperature increases oxygen-carrying capacity
questions.answer: They are biological catalysts that speed up chemical reactions; excessive heat denatures them, destroying their function
questions.explanation: Enzymes are proteins that act as biological catalysts — they speed up specific chemical reactions in living organisms without being consumed. Each enzyme has an active site that fits a specific substrate (lock-and-key model). Enzymes operate optimally at specific temperature and pH ranges. When temperature rises too high (or pH changes too much), the protein unfolds (denatures) and loses its shape — permanently destroying function. This is why a fever above 41°C (106°F) is dangerous: it denatures enzymes essential for metabolism.
questions.memory_hook: Enzymes = biological catalysts. Lock-and-key: each enzyme fits one substrate. Body temperature 37°C is optimal for most human enzymes. Denature above ~41°C — structure unfolds, function lost. That's why high fever is dangerous: your own enzymes stop working. Cooking food denatures proteins too — that's why a raw egg becomes solid when fried.
